necessity.question: Does explicit Sonnet Deep recall beat paired Fast recall while meeting the registered user latency, cost, reliability, and no-truncation thresholds?
necessity.why_now: The build-once controller, exact 670-resource no-model proof, independent review, and full repository gate are complete. No larger campaign question can be answered responsibly before this feasibility result.
necessity.why_not_smaller: The answer-blind n=12 manifest is already frozen across both domains and five abilities; reducing it now would change the registered gate and discard required coverage.
necessity.why_not_larger: n=100-300 and full-500 add substantial construction, model, reader, and judge cost before basic UX feasibility is known. They are explicitly unauthorized.

reusable_work.per_case: construct one immutable pre-query database bank, then clone the same bank into Fast and Sonnet query-only arms

expected_information_gain.primary: paired official mean Sonnet-minus-Fast score delta over 12 cases
expected_information_gain.decision_value: determines whether explicit Deep is accurate, responsive, and inexpensive enough to justify a separately preregistered confirmation

pre_dispatch_stop_predicates: stop if any hard maximum drifts upward
stop if the manifest, selection, run order, model, provider, prompt, config, or liability binding drifts
stop if all 12 materialized case contracts and pairing proofs are not complete
stop if the just-in-time endpoint inventory, credentials, packaged binaries, context preflight, or cleanup preflight fails
stop if the output root is not fresh and immutable
stop if any model dispatch would occur before its reservation and proof are durable
execution_stop_predicates: preserve and stop on any failed pair, cap, infrastructure, security, write, settlement, or proof predicate
never rerun a completed billable row
stop after the registered Sonnet decision; do not run Luna or Sol
if Sonnet is infeasible, preserve the negative artifact and stop P1-T6
if Sonnet is feasible, authorize only preparation of a separate n=100-300 confirmation manifest

just_in_time_work_deferred_until_dispatc.reason: Endpoint availability and provider routing are time-sensitive; upstream acquisition, materialization, endpoint inventory, credentials, context, and packaged-binary preflight should run once immediately before dispatch rather than be repeated early.
just_in_time_work_deferred_until_dispatc.required_command_family: scripts/run_lme_v2_p1_t6.py preflight plus registered context, reader-route, and endpoint checks

larger_run_policy.n100_to_n300: later and only after n=12 passes; requires fresh answer-blind preregistration, paired confidence procedure, spend ceiling, and fixed or adaptive stopping rule
larger_run_policy.full_500: later in campaign order after P1-T6 confirmation, P1-T1, and SWE-ContextBench Lite
larger_run_policy.method: Use information-aware selection and sequential stopping for larger confirmations; do not brute-force model grids.

claim_boundary: This checkpoint is a development control. It is not a P1-T6 pass, SOTA claim, default routing decision, ledger promotion, merge, push, or authority for n=100-300 or full-500 execution.
